queries.name: Cell Tower Cascade Failure Detection with Z-Score Analysis
queries.pain_point: Cell tower handoff cascade failures causing mass service disruption
queries.description: Detects abnormal spike patterns in cell ID failures using statistical z-score analysis across 5-minute intervals to identify cascade failures before they escalate
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_signaling_failures | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 2 hours | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(5 minutes, @timestamp) | STATS failure_count = COUNT(*), unique_imsi = COUNT_DISTINCT(imsi) BY time_bucket, current_cell_id | LOOKUP JOIN cell_tower_reference ON current_cell_id=cell_id | INLINESTATS avg_failures = AVG(failure_count), stddev_failures = STD_DEV(failure_count), p95_failures = PERCENTILE(failure_count, 95) BY cell_tower_reference.region | EVAL z_score = (failure_count - avg_failures) / COALESCE(stddev_failures, 1) | WHERE z_score > 3 AND failure_count >= 200 | EVAL severity = CASE(z_score > 5, "CRITICAL", z_score > 4, "HIGH", "MEDIUM") | KEEP current_cell_id, severity, cell_tower_reference.cell_name, z_score, failure_count, time_bucket, unique_imsi, cell_tower_reference.equipment_type | SORT z_score DESC | LIMIT 50

queries.name: IMSI Influx Anomaly Detection for Security Threats
queries.pain_point: Rogue network attempts, SS7 attacks, and international roaming configuration errors
queries.description: Identifies unusual subscriber influx patterns by detecting statistical anomalies in unique IMSI counts per MME host to catch security attacks early
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_signaling_failures | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 1 hour | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(5 minutes, @timestamp) | STATS unique_imsi_count = COUNT_DISTINCT(imsi), roaming_imsi = COUNT_DISTINCT(imsi) WHERE roaming_flag == true BY time_bucket, mme_host | LOOKUP JOIN mme_host_inventory ON mme_host | INLINESTATS avg_imsi = AVG(unique_imsi_count), stddev_imsi = STD_DEV(unique_imsi_count), baseline_imsi = PERCENTILE(unique_imsi_count, 50) BY mme_host_inventory.datacenter | EVAL z_score = (unique_imsi_count - avg_imsi) / COALESCE(stddev_imsi, 1), pct_deviation = ((unique_imsi_count - baseline_imsi) / baseline_imsi) * 100 | WHERE z_score > 3 AND unique_imsi_count > 400 | EVAL threat_level = CASE(z_score > 5, "CRITICAL_ATTACK", z_score > 4, "HIGH_RISK", "SUSPICIOUS") | KEEP mme_host, threat_level, mme_host_inventory.datacenter, z_score, unique_imsi_count, time_bucket, roaming_imsi, pct_deviation | SORT z_score DESC

queries.name: HSS Mass Authentication Failure Detection
queries.pain_point: HSS database corruption or synchronization issues affecting subscriber authentication
queries.description: Detects mass authentication failures indicating HSS problems by analyzing failure rates and response time anomalies across MME hosts
queries.esql_query: FROM subscriber_authentication_events | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 30 minutes | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(5 minutes, @timestamp) | STATS total_attempts = COUNT(*), failed_attempts = COUNT(*) WHERE auth_result == "FAILURE", avg_response_time = AVG(response_time_ms), affected_subscribers = COUNT_DISTINCT(imsi) BY time_bucket, mme_host | LOOKUP JOIN mme_host_inventory ON mme_host | EVAL failure_rate = (failed_attempts / total_attempts) * 100 | INLINESTATS baseline_failure_rate = AVG(failure_rate), stddev_failure_rate = STD_DEV(failure_rate) BY mme_host_inventory.datacenter | EVAL z_score = (failure_rate - baseline_failure_rate) / COALESCE(stddev_failure_rate, 1) | WHERE (failure_rate > 15 AND affected_subscribers > 500) OR z_score > 4 | EVAL severity = CASE(failure_rate > 50, "CRITICAL_HSS_OUTAGE", failure_rate > 30, "HIGH_HSS_DEGRADATION", "MEDIUM_HSS_ISSUE") | KEEP mme_host, severity, mme_host_inventory.software_version, failure_rate, affected_subscribers, time_bucket, avg_response_time, z_score | SORT failure_rate DESC

queries.name: MME Split-Brain and Signaling Storm Detection
queries.pain_point: Core network split-brain conditions and signaling storms causing extended outages
queries.description: Multi-dimensional analysis detecting split-brain scenarios by identifying simultaneous high error rates across multiple MME hosts with correlated failure patterns
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_signaling_failures | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 15 minutes | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(1 minute, @timestamp) | STATS error_rate = COUNT(*), unique_procedures = COUNT_DISTINCT(procedure_type), affected_cells = COUNT_DISTINCT(current_cell_id), affected_imsi = COUNT_DISTINCT(imsi) BY time_bucket, mme_host | LOOKUP JOIN mme_host_inventory ON mme_host | INLINESTATS avg_error_rate = AVG(error_rate), stddev_error_rate = STD_DEV(error_rate), max_error_rate = MAX(error_rate) BY mme_host_inventory.datacenter | EVAL z_score = (error_rate - avg_error_rate) / COALESCE(stddev_error_rate, 1), capacity_pct = (error_rate / mme_host_inventory.max_capacity) * 100 | WHERE z_score > 4 AND error_rate > 1000 AND unique_procedures > 5 | EVAL condition_type = CASE(affected_cells > 100 AND capacity_pct > 80, "SPLIT_BRAIN_CRITICAL", error_rate > 5000, "SIGNALING_STORM", "SEVERE_OVERLOAD") | KEEP mme_host, condition_type, mme_host_inventory.datacenter, z_score, error_rate, time_bucket, affected_imsi, affected_cells | SORT error_rate DESC | LIMIT 20

queries.name: Semantic Search for Similar Network Failure Patterns
queries.pain_point: Difficulty identifying root causes of recurring network issues across different failure scenarios
queries.description: Uses semantic search to find similar failure patterns based on natural language descriptions, helping identify recurring issues and root causes
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_signaling_failures | WHERE @timestamp >= NOW() - 24 hours AND SEMANTIC(failure_reason, "memory exhaustion software bugs resource depletion capacity overload") | LOOKUP JOIN cell_tower_reference ON current_cell_id=cell_id | STATS failure_count = COUNT(*), unique_subscribers = COUNT_DISTINCT(imsi), error_types = COUNT_DISTINCT(error_code) BY current_cell_id, cell_tower_reference.equipment_type, cell_tower_reference.region | WHERE failure_count > 100 | KEEP current_cell_id, cell_tower_reference.cell_name, cell_tower_reference.equipment_type, failure_count, unique_subscribers, cell_tower_reference.region, error_types | SORT failure_count DESC | LIMIT 25
